title: Watteau, Pilgrimage to Cythera
description: Test your knowledge on this object.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the meaning of *fête galante*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: ***Pilgrimage to Cythera* depicts aristocrats enjoying themselves in a fantasy like outdoor setting.   **

When Watteau presented *Pilgrimage to Cythera* to the Academy, they invented the category of a *fête galante* to refer to scenes of outdoor entertainments for the aristocracy.  

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/rococo/v/watteau-cythera)**

choices.content: a type of outdoor entertainment for the aristocracy
a period of French aristocratic rule before the Revolution
a mythological island associated with Aphrodite
a painting style that emphasizes color and soft lines
an art exhibition that was popular among aristocrats

assessment_items.question_content:
**What visual detail in this painting supports the interpretation that the figures are on their way to island of Cythera?**  

![Rococo painting of elegantly dressed figures in a lush outdoor setting, gathering for leisure and romance beneath trees and an open sky.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eef935dbd553d18d4590dd5fced61fa8.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**While scholars debate the exact moment depicted in the painting, the island in the distance that is surrounded by putti supports one theory that the figures are setting out on their way to Cythera.  **


**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/rococo/v/watteau-cythera)**

choices.content: ![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/680513dcce01d652825fd197908613de.jpg)
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/edd339854d49aa3c989b96d6a2212b6e.jpg)
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0d4c97a6bd0fe3f622be36fbbc89f34d.jpg)
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a597ef81e304663c6b15700236155ec.jpg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What about this painting seems to reflect Watteau's personal interest in opera and theater?**

![Rococo painting of elegantly dressed figures in a lush outdoor setting, gathering for leisure and romance beneath trees and an open sky.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eef935dbd553d18d4590dd5fced61fa8.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Watteau suggests that love is like a dance by showing the figures physically interacting with one other in different ways.  **

**Watteau's use of dance-like postures to show interaction between the figures reflects his interest in opera and theater, and would have appealed to aristocrats accustomed to formal dance movements.  **


**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/rococo/v/watteau-cythera)**

choices.content: The dance-like way the couples interact
The curving lines and sensuous painting style
The mythological subject matter
The emphasis on the aristocratic lifestyle
The sensual treatment of the painted landscape

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did Watteau paint *Pilgrimage to Cythera*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Artists were required to submit a reception piece prior to their acceptance into the French Royal Academy.  Although different from established genres, Watteau submitted *Pilgrimage to Cythera,* which was seen as so important that the Academy created a new category for its inclusion.  **

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/rococo/v/watteau-cythera)**

choices.content: It was a reception piece for the French Academy.
It was a private commission from the King.
It was an experimental composition intended for himself.
It was his account of an aristocratic party he attended.
It was a private commission from a French nobleman.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The use of color and open brushwork in this detail reflects Watteau's affinity with which camp of French Academic artists?**


![Alt text: Painting detail showing a group of finely dressed figures under a sky with cherubs, gathered near a statue entwined with flowers.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/82a3b9df155a3c4637682b5525ced26d.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Ancien Regime is a term used to describe the monarchy in its final period of rule prior to the French Revolution of 1789.

In contrast to the Poussinistes' interest in line and finish, Watteau and other Rubenistes emphasized color and open brushwork in their compositions.  

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/rococo/v/watteau-cythera)**

choices.content: the Rubenistes
the Poussinistes
the Ancien Regime
the Aristocracy
the Rococo

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which features of this painting are characteristic of the Rococo period of art?**

![Rococo painting of elegantly dressed figures in a lush outdoor setting, gathering for leisure and romance beneath trees and an open sky.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eef935dbd553d18d4590dd5fced61fa8.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Watteau's painting is characteristic of the Rococo period in all these ways.  

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1700-1800-age-of-enlightenment/rococo/v/watteau-cythera)**

choices.content: The depiction of the aristocratic lifestyle
The playful treatment of love and sexuality
The lush and sensuous painting style
The theme of the fête galante
